fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no APC (DOAJ, CC BY-NC-ND)
warning: Anomalous metric growth: JIF 13.4 → 11.8 ranks a small 3-issue/yr journal ~3/180 in Mechanical Eng. — citation concentration far above peers. Not on Clarivate's suppression list, but scrutinize.
note: Broad mechanical scope (mechanics, tribology, mechatronics) since 1994; CiteScore 6.1.
Rise verified as citation-driven: self-cites ~20/yr, stable ~28 papers/yr. Strongest metrics in the table.

journal: Engineering Science and Technology, an Int. J. (JESTECH)
fee_detail: US$2,000 excl. taxes (Elsevier) — formerly free, subsidy ended
warning: Shifted from no-fee to US$2,000 APC; rapid growth in visibility (CiteScore 11.3) — monitor.
note: Karabuk University's fully-OA engineering flagship, Q1 across seven Scopus categories. Exception entry: Elsevier-published.

journal: Journal of Civil Engineering and Management
note: Fully university-published since 2018 (left Taylor & Francis). Construction management, IT in construction.

fee_detail: ♦ Society-subsidized — no APC up to 8 pp. (KNS pays); US$200/page beyond
note: Gold-OA international journal across nuclear science and technology. Exception entry: society-owned, Elsevier-distributed.

publisher: Vilnius University + Lithuanian Academy of Sciences
note: Optimization, recognition, control, automation. Don't confuse with Informatica (Slovenia, Q3).

journal: Eksploatacja i Niezawodność — Maintenance and Reliability
warning: APC roughly doubled recently (older sources still show ~€410); third-party sites advertise very short turnarounds not confirmed officially.
note: Applied maintenance, reliability and diagnostics; now a strong SCIE title — JCR Q1 Engineering Multidisciplinary.

journal: JSDEWES — J. of Sustainable Development of Energy, Water and Environment Systems
note: Energy systems, smart infrastructure — strong fit for energy/edge work.

journal: Strojniški vestnik — J. of Mechanical Engineering
publisher: Assoc. of Mechanical Engineers and Technicians of Slovenia
note: Strongest mechanical venue; publishing since 1955.

publisher: Technical University of Košice (Faculty of Mining, Ecology, Process Control)
fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no submission or processing charges (DOAJ + site)
warning: SJR 2025 slipped to 0.342 (Q3) — the Q2 tier now rests on CiteScore alone; recheck at next release.
note: Mining, geology, earth resources, geotechnics since 1996. Q2 via CiteScore (Geology, Geotechnical Eng.); 5-yr IF 2.2 vs current 1.4 suggests a citation peak has passed.
AI, pattern recognition, signal/image processing, control. In DOAJ.
Flagship transport-science journal (est. 1986): road/rail/water/air transport, logistics, safety. Q2 via Scopus/SJR; JCR places it Q4.

journal: Journal of Applied and Computational Mechanics
fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no APC (official site + DOAJ)
note: Applied/computational solid and fluid mechanics; CiteScore 4.7, SNIP 1.26 — strongest diamond-OA mechanics venue after FUME.
Growth 48→83 papers/yr matched by citations; low self-citation — organic. Q2 via SJR; JCR places it Q3.

journal: International Journal of Renewable Energy Development (IJRED)
fee_detail: US$350 on acceptance; no submission fee (CC BY-SA)
warning: Fast stated turnaround (8 wks to publication) — above the red-flag line but monitor alongside output growth.
note: SINTA-1-accredited; biomass, solar, wind, renewable policy/technology. Q2 via 2024 CiteScore (2.7, 67th pct); SJR 2025 rose to 0.485 = Q2, strengthening the tier.

journal: Int. J. of Computers Communications & Control (IJCCC)
fee_detail: Gold OA, CC BY-NC · fees: verify
note: No special issues; active fraud-warning notice — integrity-conscious. Q2+ only via CiteScore (Q1 Comp. Theory & Math, Q2 Networks).
Electronics, telecom, ICT. Exception entry: institute-owned, Wiley-distributed.

journal: Int. J. of Applied Mathematics and Computer Science (AMCS)
note: Control theory, AI methods, applied math; ~50 papers/yr, low self-citation.

publisher: Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME)
fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no APC, submission or publication fees (CC BY)
note: Long-running (est. 1957) BME journal; SJR Q2 in Civil & Structural and Engineering (misc.), Q3 Geotechnical.

warning: High volume (~408 articles in 2024) for a society journal — no delisting history, but monitor volume growth and self-citation.
note: Ecological engineering, land reclamation, waste management, environmental technology; strong CEE + Asian author base.
Broad polytechnic scope: engineering, informatics, applied sciences.
Software engineering, information systems.

journal: Frattura ed Integrità Strutturale / Fracture and Structural Integrity
publisher: Gruppo Italiano Frattura (Italian Group of Fracture, IGF)
fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no APC since launch (2007), explicit IGF policy
note: Fracture mechanics, fatigue, structural integrity; CiteScore 3.4 (Q2, 55th pct Mechanics of Materials).
Control, robotics, signal processing, computer vision. 0% self-citation 2024–25. Exception entry: T&F-distributed.

journal: Turkish J. of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences
note: Bimonthly; control, AI, signal processing, networks. Q2 via SJR only — journal's own site honestly lists JCR Q3.
8 issues/yr, ~170 papers, ~30% rejection rate (official). Sustainable technology focus; univ. announced Q1 Multidisciplinary (SJR Apr 2024 release).

journal: ETASR — Engineering, Technology & Applied Science Research
warning: Very high volume (5,000+ papers, strong growth) — indexed and legitimate, but monitor per screening rules.

journal: Latin American Journal of Solids and Structures (LAJSS)
publisher: Brazilian Society of Mechanical Sciences and Engineering (ABCM)
speed_official: Paid fast track: publication within 35 d (€1,200)
warning: Paid ultra-fast track (35 d for €1,200) is an integrity red flag; SCImago lists an individual, not the society, as publisher of record — double-check governance.
note: SCIE-indexed solids/structures journal on SciELO; Q2 in Aerospace/Automotive/Ocean/Mechanical Eng. (2024).
All engineering branches incl. CS, electronics, energy. Exception entry: Springer-distributed.

speed_official: 14 d first decision · 150 d median final (official)
note: Broad engineering + CS; official speed transparency. Q2 via JCR and CiteScore; SJR says Q3.

journal: Bulletin of the Polish Academy of Sciences: Technical Sciences
fee_detail: 2,000 PLN (~€500) up to 8 pp., +250 PLN/page (official, from Jan 2025)
note: Q2 only via CiteScore (Engineering, 55th pct); broad technical scope incl. AI, networks.

journal: GRAĐEVINAR — J. of the Croatian Association of Civil Engineers
fee_detail: ♦ No APC; per-page English proofreading (~€8) or translation (€14.50–16.50) costs after acceptance
note: Monthly bilingual (HR/EN) society journal on construction, structures, materials; SJR 0.366 is its historical best. JCR places it Q4.

publisher: University of Žilina, Faculty of Civil Engineering
warning: APC raised ~54% in one year; aggregators advertise ~8-wk submission-to-publication (unconfirmed officially).
note: Civil engineering, transport structures, environmental engineering. Exception entry: Sciendo (De Gruyter)-distributed.
Self-citation only 5.9% — cleanest integrity profile in Tier 2.

publisher: Institute of Advanced Engineering and Science
warning: Advertises 1–2 week first decision — close to the review-speed red flag; very high IAES volume. Weakest integrity profile in this table. Claims CiteScore Q2 on its own channels — treat as marketing until checked on Scopus.

journal: International Arab Journal of Information Technology
publisher: Zarqa University / Colleges of Computing & Info. Society
note: Quarterly; CiteScore 4.2.

publisher: University of Zagreb, Faculty of Transport and Traffic Sciences
fee_detail: €1,200 + 25% VAT (€1,500 total, official); explicitly no waivers
warning: SJR quartile disputed across sources (Q2 on some mirrors, Q3 on others) — reclassified conservatively to Tier 2. High APC with no waiver policy, plus unusual €500 withdrawal fee and €500 'ethical breach penalty fee'.
note: Traffic engineering, transport planning, logistics, ITS since 1989; SJR 0.321 is its historical peak. JCR places it Q4.

warning: JCR 2024 places it Q4 in Environmental Sciences (~300/358); qualifies only via SJR Q3 — borderline, recheck at next release.
note: SCIE-indexed PAS quarterly on air/water/soil pollution control and environmental engineering.

warning: High volume (~241 papers/yr) and 20% self-citation (2023–24) — indexed but apply your screening.
note: Bimonthly, multidisciplinary engineering.
Sensors, measurement systems, signal processing, embedded — good fit for instrumentation-adjacent work.
Heat transfer, HVAC, combustion, applied thermal systems; Scopus + ESCI since 2015. CiteScore ~2.0 (low-mid Q3).

publisher: PAS Committee of Automatic Control and Robotics
note: Control theory, optimization, decision support, robotics.

publisher: PAS Committee for Civil and Water Engineering (with Warsaw UT)
fee_detail: 4,850 PLN gross / €1,150 for foreign authors from 1 Jan 2026 (was 2,780 PLN — official Guidelines for Authors)
warning: Fee raised ~75% effective Jan 2026; some directories (DOAJ) still show the old 2,780 PLN figure.
note: Structural, geotechnical, materials and construction-management research; CiteScore 1.7. Large issues (up to ~40 papers).

publisher: Institute for Research and Design in Industry
note: Energy efficiency and renewables focus; Serbian ministry-ranked M24.

journal: The Baltic Journal of Road and Bridge Engineering
publisher: Riga Technical University (with VILNIUS TECH, TalTech, Baltic Road Association)
fee_detail: €800 from Dec 2025 (€600 from Dec 2024); formerly free
warning: Rapid fee escalation: sponsor-funded no-fee OA → €600 → €800 within a year; legacy pages still advertise 'free of charges'.
note: Joint Baltic quarterly on road and bridge engineering — pavements, structures, materials, asset management. CiteScore 1.6.

publisher: PAS Committee on Production Engineering + Polish Association for Production Management
fee_detail: €200 + VAT for 10 pp., +€20/extra page (max 18); no submission charge
note: Production engineering, operations, industrial management; all five Scopus categories Q3 (2024).

fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no APC or submission charges (CC BY)
note: PAS quarterly on most branches of mechanical engineering (est. 1954); now self-published on journals.pan.pl (left De Gruyter). CiteScore 2.0.
Cheapest APC among verified Scopus/SCIE EEE venues.

publisher: PAS Committee on Thermodynamics and Combustion + IMP PAN
fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no APC (PAS/IMP-funded, CC BY-NC-ND)
warning: Quartile has oscillated Q3/Q4 (SJR dipped 2021) — recheck at 2025 SJR release.
note: Classical and applied thermodynamics, heat transfer, power cycles; ESCI since 2015, JIF since 2022.

journal: IJECES — Int. J. of Electrical and Computer Engineering Systems
note: Young venue (Scopus 2016+), H-index 12.

publisher: University of Latvia, Inst. of Mathematics and Informatics
speed_official: Immediate online publication of accepted papers (official)
note: Q3 by SJR only; small venue (H-index 9), Baltic-scope.

journal: Advances in Electrical and Computer Engineering
publisher: Ștefan cel Mare Univ. of Suceava
note: 3 issues/yr since 2025 (shrinking, not inflating).
Applied information processing, networks.

journal: Journal of ICT Research and Applications
note: Q3 by SJR only; JCR places it Q4 — borderline entry.

journal: Int. J. of Electronics and Telecommunications
publisher: PAS Committee of Electronics and Telecommunications
note: Cheapest of the PAS set.
RF, wireless, signal processing.

publisher: ICAT, Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM)
fee_detail: ♦ Diamond OA — no submission, processing or colour charges (UNAM-funded)
warning: Legacy Elsevier pages mark it 'discontinued' — that refers only to ended Elsevier distribution (~2017); the journal is active (Vol. 24, 2026). No current JCR IF found — verify WoS status if it matters.
note: Bimonthly UNAM engineering/applied-technology journal in English; Scopus Q3 Engineering (misc.).

journal: South African Journal of Industrial Engineering
publisher: Southern African Institute for Industrial Engineering (SAIIE)

note: Accredited SAIIE society journal; industrial engineering research with Southern African focus.

Optical sensors, image processing, photonics; CiteScore 5.0, SNIP 0.97.
